images.caption:
Figure 1:Sentence-level encoding of a chest X-ray report.Each visit pairs a radiograph with a free-text report composed of a
Findings and an Impression section. We split both sections into
individual sentences and embed each sentence independently with the
frozen pre-trainedall-mpnet-base-v2sentence transformer,
producing onedd-dimensional vector per sentence. The resulting
unordered collection of sentence embeddingsℰ​(r)={𝐞1F,…,𝐞5F,𝐞1I,𝐞2I}⊂ℝd\mathcal{E}(r)=\{\mathbf{e}^{F}_{1},\dots,\mathbf{e}^{F}_{5},\mathbf{e}^{I}_{1},\mathbf{e}^{I}_{2}\}\subset\mathbb{R}^{d}serves
as the report representation throughout Sec.3.

(a)Transition computation. A directional set distance𝒯\mathcal{T}reduces the (prior, current) sentence-embedding
sets to a single vectorg(k)∈ℝd′g^{(k)}\in\mathbb{R}^{d^{\prime}}.

(a)Transition computation. A directional set distance𝒯\mathcal{T}reduces the (prior, current) sentence-embedding
sets to a single vectorg(k)∈ℝd′g^{(k)}\in\mathbb{R}^{d^{\prime}}.
